channel.title: go on Abhishek's blog
channel.description: Recent content in go on Abhishek's blog

item.title: Go and Redis, Better Together 🤝
item.description: I recently presented this talk at the Conf42 Golang 2023 and I thought it might be a good idea to turn it into a blog post for folks who don’t want to spend 40+ mins watching the talk (it’s ok, I understand 😉) or just staring at slides trying to imagine what I was saying.

item.title: Use AWS Controllers for Kubernetes to deploy a Serverless data processing solution with SQS, Lambda and DynamoDB
item.description: In this blog post, you will be using AWS Controllers for Kubernetes on an Amazon EKS cluster to put together a solution wherein data from an Amazon SQS queue is processed by an AWS Lambda function and persisted to a DynamoDB table.

item.title: Use Golang for data processing with Amazon Kinesis and AWS Lambda
item.description: This blog post is for folks interested in learning how to use Golang and AWS Lambda to build a serverless solution.

item.title: Deploying Go Applications to AWS App Runner: A Step-by-Step Guide
item.description: …With the managed runtime for Go In this blog post you will learn how to run a Go application to AWS App Runner using the Go platform runtime.

item.title: Use CDK to deploy a complete solution with Kafka, App Runner, EKS and DynamoDB
item.description: A previous blog post covered how to deploy a Go Lambda function and trigger it in response to events sent to a topic in a MSK Serverless cluster.

item.title: Getting started with MSK Serverless and AWS Lambda using Go
item.description: In this blog post you will learn how to deploy a Go Lambda function and trigger it in response to events sent to a topic in a MSK Serverless cluster.

item.title: Kubexpose: A Kubernetes Operator, for fun and profit!
item.description: Say you have a web service running as a Kubernetes Deployment. There are a bunch of ways to access it over a public URL, but Kubexpose makes it easy to do so.

item.title: Redis Streams in Action - Part 4 (Serverless Go app to monitor tweets processor)
item.description: Welcome to this series of blog posts which covers Redis Streams with the help of a practical example. We will use a sample application to make Twitter data available for search and query in real-time.

item.title: Redis Streams in Action - Part 3 (Java app to process tweets with Redis Streams)
item.description: Welcome to this series of blog posts which covers Redis Streams with the help of a practical example. We will use a sample application to make Twitter data available for search and query in real-time.

item.title: Redis Streams in Action — Part 2 (Tweets consumer app)
item.description: Welcome to this series of blog posts which covers Redis Streams with the help of a practical example. We will use a sample application to make Twitter data available for search and query in real-time.

item.title: Redis Streams in Action: Part 1 (Intro and overview)
item.description: Welcome to this series of blog posts which covers Redis Streams with the help of a practical example. We will use a sample application to make Twitter data available for search and query in real-time.

item.title: RediSearch in Action
item.description: Redis has a versatile set of data structures ranging from simple Strings all the way to powerful abstractions such as Redis Streams.

item.title: Build fault tolerant applications with Cassandra API for Azure Cosmos DB
item.description: Azure Cosmos DB is a resource governed system that allows you to execute a certain number of operations per second based on the provisioned throughput you have configured.
